content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all four pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Threaded MilestoneJournalPort through rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal and load_planned_elsewhere_mappings instead of hardcoding FsMilestoneJournalStore — the journal store is now injectable, enabling testability with in-memory stores. Updated all 10 call sites (5 in planned_elsewhere.rs tests, 4 in service.rs tests, 1 in success_reconciliation.rs). Amendment 2: Changed the run_id filter in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success from is_none_or (which included legacy None-run_id mappings) to exact match m.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id), so only mappings explicitly stamped with the current run are processed. Legacy None-run_id mappings are now skipped, preventing cross-run re-verification and duplicate br comments. Amendment 3: Added completion_round: Option<u32> to PlannedElsewhereMapping with serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for backward compatibility. The engine's record_planned_elsewhere_amendments now accepts and stamps completion_round (cursor.completion_round on Complete path, from_round on Restart path). Journal metadata includes completion_round when present, and rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal extracts it. Reconstruction in success_reconciliation stamps the round from the aggregate payload. Amendment 4: Replaced the PlannedElsewhereMapped event type in journal writes with ProgressUpdated carrying a sub_type='planned_elsewhere_mapped' metadata discriminator, so rolled-back code (which knows ProgressUpdated but not PlannedElsewhereMapped) can still parse the journal. The rebuild reader now recognizes both legacy PlannedElsewhereMapped events and the new ProgressUpdated+sub_type pattern. Also made read_journal_from_path in fs.rs lenient — it now skips unparseable lines with a tracing::warn instead of returning CorruptRecord, providing defense-in-depth for any future event types or schema changes.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Changed rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal signature to accept journal_store: &impl MilestoneJournalPort instead of hardcoding FsMilestoneJournalStore. Removed the use crate::adapters::fs::FsMilestoneJournalStore import from the function body. Added journal_store parameter to load_planned_elsewhere_mappings and threaded it through to the rebuild function.
2. [Completed] Amendment 1: Updated all 10 call sites of load_planned_elsewhere_mappings to pass &FsMilestoneJournalStore as the new journal_store parameter — 5 in planned_elsewhere.rs tests, 4 in service.rs tests, and 1 in success_reconciliation.rs verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success.
3. [Completed] Amendment 2: Changed the run_id filter in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success from m.run_id.as_deref().is_none_or(|rid| rid == run_id) to m.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id), requiring an exact match. Legacy None-run_id mappings are now excluded from verification, preventing cross-run re-verification and duplicate br comments.
4. [Completed] Amendment 3a: Added completion_round: Option<u32> field to PlannedElsewhereMapping in model.rs with #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] for backward-compatible serialization.
5. [Completed] Amendment 3b: Added completion_round to journal metadata persistence in record_planned_elsewhere_mapping (service.rs) when present, and extraction in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal via metadata.get("completion_round").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).map(|v| v as u32).
6. [Completed] Amendment 3c: Added completion_round parameter to record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs. Updated both call sites: Complete path passes cursor.completion_round, Restart path passes from_round. The mapping construction now includes completion_round: Some(completion_round).
7. [Completed] Amendment 3d: Updated all PlannedElsewhereMapping construction sites with completion_round field — planned_elsewhere.rs (None), success_reconciliation.rs verified mapping (inherits from outcome), reconstruction (Some(round)), and 8 test construction sites in service.rs (None).
8. [Completed] Amendment 4a: Changed record_planned_elsewhere_mapping in service.rs to write PE journal events as MilestoneEventType::ProgressUpdated instead of PlannedElsewhereMapped, with an additional sub_type='planned_elsewhere_mapped' metadata discriminator. This ensures rolled-back code that does not know PlannedElsewhereMapped can still parse the journal line.
9. [Completed] Amendment 4b: Updated rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal to recognize both the legacy PlannedElsewhereMapped event type AND ProgressUpdated events where metadata sub_type == 'planned_elsewhere_mapped'. This provides bidirectional compatibility for journals containing either format.
10. [Completed] Amendment 4c: Made read_journal_from_path in fs.rs lenient — changed from serde_json::from_str with map_err returning CorruptRecord to a match that pushes Ok events and logs Err with tracing::warn, skipping unparseable lines instead of failing. Renamed milestone_id parameter to _milestone_id since it is no longer used in error messages.
11. [Completed] Amendment 4d: Updated test assertions in planned_elsewhere.rs that checked for PlannedElsewhereMapped journal events to instead check for ProgressUpdated events with sub_type='planned_elsewhere_mapped' metadata. Updated both the successful_mapping_with_verified_bead test and the persistence_survives_reload test.
12. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix formatting differences (filter closure collapsed to single line, MilestoneJournalEvent::new chain reformatted, load_planned_elsewhere_mappings calls expanded to multi-line).
13. [Completed] Ran cargo test (1617 passed: 684 lib + 174 integration + 1 tmux + 758 unit, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 1617 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (684 lib, 174 integration, 1 tmux, 758 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (service.rs, success_reconciliation.rs, planned_elsewhere.rs, model.rs, engine.rs, fs.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal signature now accepts journal_store: &impl MilestoneJournalPort; the FsMilestoneJournalStore import was removed from the function body; all 10 call sites pass &FsMilestoneJournalStore as the journal store parameter
- Amendment 2 verified: filter in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success at line ~721 now uses m.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id) — exact match only, no is_none_or fallback. Legacy None-run_id mappings are excluded from verification.
- Amendment 3 verified: PlannedElsewhereMapping.completion_round is Option<u32> with serde(default) for backward compatibility. Engine stamps completion_round on production mappings via cursor.completion_round (Complete) and from_round (Restart). Journal metadata includes completion_round when present. Rebuild extracts it. Reconstruction stamps Some(round) from the aggregate's completion_round.
- Amendment 4 verified: record_planned_elsewhere_mapping now writes ProgressUpdated event type with sub_type='planned_elsewhere_mapped' metadata. rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal checks for both PlannedElsewhereMapped and ProgressUpdated+sub_type patterns. read_journal_from_path skips unparseable lines with tracing::warn instead of returning CorruptRecord. Tests updated to assert ProgressUpdated event type with sub_type metadata.
- Files modified: src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs (completion_round field), src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs (journal port threading, completion_round metadata, ProgressUpdated write, dual-format read), src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (exact run_id filter, journal port call site, completion_round on verified/reconstructed mappings), src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs (completion_round: None, journal port in test call sites, updated event type assertions), src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs (completion_round parameter + stamping), src/adapters/fs.rs (lenient journal reader)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- One flaky integration test (checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_includes_*) occasionally fails due to git timing; unrelated to this change and non-reproducible in isolation.
- The lenient journal reader in fs.rs silently skips unparseable lines. If a journal file is genuinely corrupt (not just containing unknown event types), the corruption will be silently absorbed rather than surfaced. The tracing::warn provides observability but operators must monitor logs.
- The ProgressUpdated event type used for PE journal events means old code that reads ProgressUpdated events will see PE events as harmless progress updates. If old code takes action on ProgressUpdated events (e.g., updating progress counters), PE events could inflate those counts. In practice, ProgressUpdated events carry metadata and are typically logged without side effects.
- The completion_round field on PlannedElsewhereMapping is not yet used for active filtering during verification — it provides provenance for future round-staleness logic. The reconstruction path already uses round-level dedup (latest_by_round HashMap) which prevents stale-round aggregates from being reconstructed, but journal-persisted mappings from earlier rounds within the same run are still included in the verification set.
- The reconcile_planned_elsewhere function (used in tests and direct reconciliation, not production orchestration) does not have run_id or completion_round context and stamps both as None. This is acceptable because the production path through record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs always provides both fields.
